medications.indication_rationale: Overdue given more than 10 years since the last booster, and this is a contaminated (kitchen) wound.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Continue firm direct pressure on the wound with a clean cloth and keep the arm elevated above heart level while arranging urgent transport for wound closure.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Controls ongoing bleeding and reduces swelling while awaiting definitive care.

patient_education_points.point: This cut is deep enough that it needs to be professionally cleaned and stitched, ideally within the next few hours, rather than just bandaged at home.
Please go to your GP (if they can do procedures) or the Emergency Department soon for wound closure.
Keep firm pressure on it and keep your arm raised until you're seen.
You'll likely need a tetanus booster since it's been over 10 years since your last one.
If the bleeding becomes uncontrollable despite firm pressure, or you develop new numbness or can't move your fingers, seek emergency care immediately.

follow_up_plan.interval: Same-day in-person wound closure, ideally within a few hours
follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Wound healing, signs of infection, and suture/staple removal timing
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: Uncontrollable bleeding, new numbness, or inability to move the fingers — seek emergency care immediately.

must_include_items: Same-day in-person wound closure recommendation (GP with procedural skills or ED)
Direct pressure and elevation advice while awaiting care
Safety-netting for uncontrolled bleeding or new neurological deficit
acceptable_alternatives: Either GP with suturing capability or ED, depending on availability and wait times
errors_of_omission: No recommendation for in-person wound closure
errors_of_commission: Recommending only home bandaging without any in-person plan
Escalating unnecessarily to an ambulance for a stable, controlled wound
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: Recommending only home management for a deep, gaping wound with visible fat exposure is a moderate safety/quality failure given infection and scarring risk.
